stock_market.summary: KOSPI closed March with a 3.00% decline to 5119, capping a volatile month impacted by Middle East tensions and currency depreciation. Markets are looking toward South Korea's WGBI inclusion on April 1st as a potential catalyst for stability and foreign capital inflow.

key_metrics.market_trend: Downbeat quarter-end, volatility driven by geopolitical risks
key_metrics.impact: Korean won at 17-year low, eyes on April WGBI inclusion

art_market.summary: The art market maintains a quality-focused resilience as it enters April. Despite broader macro uncertainties, blue-chip contemporary works remain in steady demand, with collectors prioritizing liquidity and provenance.

key_metrics.major_events: Consolidation phase post-March auctions, emphasis on asset quality
